bins.name: Faster interconnection study process
bins.desc: Oracle supports procedural reforms to speed FERC's own decision-making, including shot clocks and shorter comment periods. The filing links energy access delays directly to slower AI infrastructure deployment.
quotes: One of the most significant and widespread delays Oracle encounters when siting data centers in the United States is access to sufficient energy.
The Commission should support efforts to streamline administrative procedures and harmonize standards related to load sheds and down time.
the Commission could explore imposing decision shot clocks on its own cases.

bins.name: Harmonized load shedding rules
bins.desc: Oracle asks FERC to harmonize load shedding practices and downtime calculations across regions, arguing that the current patchwork of standards creates unnecessary friction for data center operators.
quotes: The Commission should support efforts to streamline administrative procedures and harmonize standards related to load sheds and down time.
the Commission could encourage ways to harmonize load shedding practices as well as downtime calculations.

bins.name: Energy access as key barrier
bins.desc: Oracle identifies insufficient energy access as the single most significant and widespread obstacle it faces when siting U.S. data centers, framing the rulemaking as an opportunity to close that gap.
quotes: One of the most significant and widespread delays Oracle encounters when siting data centers in the United States is access to sufficient energy.

bins.name: Uniform national standards
bins.desc: Oracle argues that a patchwork of regional load shedding and downtime rules slows data center deployment and asks FERC to encourage harmonized national standards.
quotes: The Commission should support efforts to streamline administrative procedures and harmonize standards related to load sheds and down time.
the Commission could encourage ways to harmonize load shedding practices as well as downtime calculations.

bins.name: Shot clocks and shorter proceedings
bins.desc: Oracle requests that FERC impose decision shot clocks, shorten comment periods, limit intervenor rounds to one, and streamline backstop permitting to cut the time from application to decision.
quotes: the Commission could explore imposing decision shot clocks on its own cases.
Shortening longer comment periods and limiting intervenor and reply comment rounds to a single round will allow for public participation without creating a heckler's veto.
the Commission could explore ways to streamline backstop permitting procedures.

bins.name: AI race and U.S. dominance
bins.desc: Oracle frames the entire filing around U.S. AI competitiveness, arguing that powering data centers fast is essential to winning the AI race against global rivals.
quotes: Winning the AI race with an American tech stack requires powering data centers here in the United States and abroad as fast as possible.
